Tapi Erica, aku juga ingin mengatakan, seperti yang kita bahas sejam ini, di peringatan 250 tahun Amerika, pengunjung yang datang ke ibu kota negara kita akan melihat pemandangan seperti ini, menurutku ini sangat menyedihkan.

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: But to end on a hopeful note, the US Catholic Bishops did just consecrate the United States to the Sacred Heart of Jesus. So maybe we could all just use a moment to reflect on some of the positive things that are happening in our country as this anniversary approaches.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi untuk mengakhiri dengan penuh harapan, para uskup Katolik Amerika baru saja mendedikasikan Amerika kepada Hati Kudus Yesus. Jadi mungkin kita semua bisa meluangkan waktu untuk merenungkan beberapa hal positif yang terjadi di negara kita menjelang peringatan ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: That was an interesting turn, Rob. Maria, you've got the last 30 seconds.
paragraphs.cn: Itu perubahan topik yang menarik, Rob. Maria, kamu punya 30 detik terakhir.

paragraphs.speaker: Cardona
paragraphs.en: You know, I think that this is something that people should take a look at and say, let's try to come together. Let's actually try to come together for the country for the 250th anniversary. Let's try to do something that is completely the opposite of Donald Trump, and actually bring people together to celebrate our freedoms, to celebrate this country and the values it was founded on.
paragraphs.cn: Aku pikir ini adalah hal yang harus diperhatikan orang dan katakan 'mari kita bersatu'. Mari kita benar-benar bersatu untuk negara, untuk peringatan 250 tahun. Mari kita lakukan sesuatu yang benar-benar berlawanan dengan Donald Trump, benar-benar menyatukan orang, merayakan kebebasan kita, merayakan negara ini dan nilai-nilai yang menjadi fondasinya.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: I would like to thank the three of you for coming together on this Friday morning, because it's always a pleasure to see all three of you. I hope you all have a great weekend. We'll see you soon. Cardona: Thanks, Erica.
paragraphs.cn: Aku ingin berterima kasih kepada kalian bertiga sudah berkumpul pagi Jumat ini, karena melihat kalian bertiga selalu menyenangkan. Semoga akhir pekanmu menyenangkan. Sampai jumpa lagi. Cardona: Terima kasih, Erica.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Thanks to all of you for joining us this morning, I'm Erica Hill. The headlines are next. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: Terima kasih semuanya sudah berpartisipasi pagi ini, aku Erica Hill. Selanjutnya berita utama.

vocabulary.en: to praise or promote something in a persistent or exaggerated way
vocabulary.excerpt: Trump Touts 'Great Settlement' with Iran, Calls Off Strikes
vocabulary.exampleCn: Trump mempromosikan 'rekonsiliasi besar' dengan Iran, membatalkan serangan

vocabulary.cn: ditinggalkan, diabaikan (terutama dalam hubungan cinta atau politik)
vocabulary.en: rejected or abandoned, especially by a lover or ally
vocabulary.excerpt: Could Jilted GOP Lawmakers Turn on Trump's Agenda?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Akankah anggota Kongres Partai Republik yang diabaikan beralih menentang agenda Trump?

vocabulary.en: a narrow passage of water connecting two seas or two large areas of water
vocabulary.excerpt: The strait will officially open as soon as we sign, which could be soon.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Selat itu akan resmi dibuka setelah kita menandatangani, mungkin segera.

vocabulary.en: to spray someone with pepper spray, a chemical irritant
vocabulary.excerpt: A suspect pepper-sprayed the victim and stole their cards after they met up for the sale.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Seorang tersangka menyemprotkan semprotan merica ke korban saat pertemuan transaksi dan mencuri kartu mereka.

vocabulary.en: a temporary suspension of fighting, typically one during which peace talks take place
vocabulary.excerpt: It may be a ceasefire that opens up another negotiation.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ini mungkin perjanjian gencatan senjata yang membuka putaran negosiasi lain.

vocabulary.en: a point on which agreement cannot be reached; a stumbling block
vocabulary.excerpt: This has been a key sticking point, as we know, for Iran.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Seperti yang kita tahu, ini selalu menjadi titik kritis bagi Iran.

vocabulary.en: to suddenly return to a difficult or dangerous situation
vocabulary.excerpt: if they don't respect that ceasefire, and it plunges everything back in again
vocabulary.exampleCn: Jika mereka tidak menghormati gencatan senjata, itu akan membuat semuanya kembali terjerumus ke dalam perang.

vocabulary.en: a heavy or continuous attack, especially with bombs or artillery
vocabulary.excerpt: 38 days of pounding the Iranians, starting in late February, did not result in the political concessions he was looking for.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 从2月底开始的38天对伊朗的猛烈打击并没有带来他所寻求的政治让步。

vocabulary.en: something that is granted, especially in response to demands; a compromise
vocabulary.excerpt: did not result in the political concessions he was looking for
vocabulary.exampleCn: 没有带来他所寻求的政治让步

vocabulary.en: a formal agreement between two or more parties, not legally binding but outlining mutual understanding
vocabulary.excerpt: according to a diplomat who's been briefed on this agreement, this memorandum of understanding
vocabulary.exampleCn: 据一位了解该协议的外交官称，这份谅解备忘录

vocabulary.en: the prevention of the spread of nuclear weapons and other weapons of mass destruction
vocabulary.excerpt: when they signed the nuclear nonproliferation treaty
vocabulary.exampleCn: 当他们签署《核不扩散条约》时

vocabulary.cn: 笼斗（综合格斗比赛形式）
vocabulary.en: a type of mixed martial arts (MMA) fight held in a cage-like enclosure
vocabulary.excerpt: Just what do cage fights actually have to do with diplomacy?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Apa hubungan antara pertarungan kandang dengan diplomasi?

vocabulary.en: Initial Public Offering; the first sale of stock by a company to the public
vocabulary.excerpt: SpaceX launching on the NASDAQ this morning.
vocabulary.exampleCn: SpaceX listing di Nasdaq pagi ini.

vocabulary.en: a person whose wealth is at least one trillion dollars
vocabulary.excerpt: Elon Musk now set to become the world's first trillionaire.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Elon Musk akan segera menjadi triliuner pertama di dunia.

vocabulary.en: to make people uncertain about something; to question the truth or validity of
vocabulary.excerpt: Republicans continuing to cast doubt on California's voting tactics.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Partai Republik terus meragukan strategi pemungutan suara California.

vocabulary.en: a violent, destructive windstorm characterized by a twisting, funnel-shaped cloud
vocabulary.excerpt: Tornadoes touch down, with severe storms heading East today.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Angin puting beliung mendarat, badai kuat bergerak ke timur hari ini.

vocabulary.en: the profession, activity, or skill of managing international relations
vocabulary.excerpt: Just what do cage fights actually have to do with diplomacy?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Apa hubungan antara pertarungan kandang dan diplomasi?

vocabulary.en: not easily convinced; having doubts
vocabulary.excerpt: So, there are some reasons to be skeptical of the president's claim.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Oleh karena itu, ada beberapa alasan untuk bersikap skeptis terhadap pernyataan presiden.

vocabulary.en: given essential information about a situation or plan
vocabulary.excerpt: according to a diplomat who's been briefed on this agreement
vocabulary.exampleCn: Menurut seorang diplomat yang mengetahui perjanjian tersebut

vocabulary.en: legally or morally obliged to follow a rule or agreement
vocabulary.excerpt: he won't feel bound by a commitment that he didn't sign up to
vocabulary.exampleCn: Dia tidak akan merasa terikat oleh janji yang tidak dia tandatangani

vocabulary.cn: panggilan telepon yang penuh pertengkaran sengit (kiasan)
vocabulary.en: very angry or heated telephone conversations
vocabulary.excerpt: There have been some screaming phone calls we've heard about along the way.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Kami mendengar beberapa panggilan telepon yang penuh pertengkaran sengit di sepanjang jalan.

vocabulary.en: to damage or weaken something, especially gradually or insidiously
vocabulary.excerpt: Is Trump undermining his efforts at this point, showing those cards?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 特朗普此时是否在削弱自己的努力，亮出底牌？

vocabulary.en: to cause persistent trouble or distress to someone
vocabulary.excerpt: the problem that's been haunting the president here is that 38 days of pounding the Iranians...
vocabulary.exampleCn: 一直困扰总统的问题是，38天对伊朗的打击...

vocabulary.en: to treat something in a casual or unserious manner; to manipulate
vocabulary.excerpt: I think California is playing around with us.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我认为加州在耍我们。

sentences.en: What started out as somebody trying to sell Pokemon cards ended in a robbery in San Francisco.
sentences.cn: 起初只是有人想卖宝可梦卡，结果却在旧金山演变成了一起抢劫案。
sentences.structure: 主句：What started out as somebody trying to sell Pokemon cards ended in a robbery in San Francisco. 主语从句：What started out as somebody trying to sell Pokemon cards
sentences.analysis: 主语从句作主语，谓语ended in表示'以……告终'。'somebody trying to sell'是动名词短语作宾语。

sentences.en: President Trump announcing a great settlement to end the war.
sentences.cn: 特朗普总统宣布达成一项结束战争的伟大协议。
sentences.structure: 独立主格结构：President Trump announcing a great settlement to end the war
sentences.analysis: 这是新闻标题式表达，省略了系动词is。'a great settlement to end the war'中不定式作定语。

sentences.en: When President Kennedy announced that we were going to put a man on the Moon and return him safely to the Earth, no one thought that was possible.
sentences.cn: 当肯尼迪总统宣布我们要把人送上月球并安全返回地球时，没有人认为这是可能的。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句：When President Kennedy announced that...；主句：no one thought that was possible；宾语从句：that we were going to put a man on the Moon and return him safely to the Earth
sentences.analysis: 多层从句嵌套。'return him safely to the Earth'与'put a man on the Moon'并列。

sentences.en: Marco Rubio comparing a deal with the UFC to the Moon landing.
sentences.cn: 马可·卢比奥将一项与UFC的协议比作登月。
sentences.structure: 独立主格结构：Marco Rubio comparing a deal with the UFC to the Moon landing
sentences.analysis: 新闻标题式表达，省略系动词。'comparing...to...'表示'把……比作……'。

sentences.en: President Trump calling off new strikes on Iran, saying both sides have now agreed to stop the fighting.
sentences.cn: 特朗普总统取消了对伊朗的新一轮打击，称双方现已同意停止战斗。
sentences.structure: 主句：President Trump calling off new strikes on Iran；伴随状语：saying both sides have now agreed to stop the fighting
sentences.analysis: 现在分词短语作伴随状语。'call off'意为'取消'。

sentences.en: Regardless of the rhetoric, threats and claims of the other side, we are focused on the interests of the Iranian nation.
sentences.cn: 无论对方的言论、威胁和主张如何，我们都专注于伊朗国家的利益。
sentences.structure: 让步状语：Regardless of the rhetoric, threats and claims of the other side；主句：we are focused on the interests of the Iranian nation
sentences.analysis: 'Regardless of'引导让步状语。'be focused on'表示'专注于'。

sentences.en: Regarding the text and the overall understanding, whenever we conclude that it secures the interests of the Iranian nation, we will certainly announce it transparently.
sentences.cn: 关于文本和总体理解，只要我们得出结论认为它保障了伊朗国家的利益，我们一定会透明地宣布。
sentences.structure: 状语：Regarding the text and the overall understanding；时间状语从句：whenever we conclude that it secures the interests of the Iranian nation；主句：we will certainly announce it transparently
sentences.analysis: 'Regarding'表示'关于'。'whenever'引导时间状语从句，从句中包含that引导的宾语从句。

sentences.en: Israel, also seemingly taken by surprise, a source telling CNN Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu wasn't aware of an agreement with Iran; that he actually found out on social media.
sentences.cn: 以色列似乎也感到意外，有消息人士告诉CNN，以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡并不知晓与伊朗的协议；他实际上是在社交媒体上得知的。
sentences.structure: 主句：Israel, also seemingly taken by surprise；同位语：a source telling CNN...；宾语从句：Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu wasn't aware of an agreement with Iran；并列宾语从句：that he actually found out on social media
sentences.analysis: 'taken by surprise'是过去分词短语作定语。'a source telling CNN'是独立主格结构。'find out'意为'发现、得知'。

sentences.en: So, is this just the latest effort by President Trump to move stalled negotiations forward?
sentences.cn: 那么，这仅仅是特朗普总统推动陷入僵局的谈判向前迈进的最新努力吗？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句：is this just the latest effort by President Trump to move stalled negotiations forward
sentences.analysis: 'stalled negotiations'意为'陷入僵局的谈判'。'move...forward'表示'推动……前进'。

sentences.en: The reopening of the Strait of Hormuz could actually happen this weekend once this deal is signed.
sentences.cn: 一旦协议签署，霍尔木兹海峡的重新开放实际上可能在本周末实现。
sentences.structure: 主句：The reopening of the Strait of Hormuz could actually happen this weekend；条件状语从句：once this deal is signed
sentences.analysis: 'once'引导条件状语从句，表示'一旦'。'the reopening of...'是名词短语作主语。

sentences.en: Based on everything we know and your sources, David, do signs actually point to an agreement at this point?
sentences.cn: 根据我们所知的一切以及你的消息来源，大卫，目前迹象是否真的指向一项协议？
sentences.structure: 状语：Based on everything we know and your sources；主句：do signs actually point to an agreement at this point
sentences.analysis: 'Based on'是过去分词短语作状语。'point to'表示'指向、表明'。

sentences.en: Well, they certainly point to some agreement coming together on four major pillars of a -- of some restraint on the nuclear program.
sentences.cn: 嗯，它们确实指向在四个主要支柱上达成某种协议 -- -- 对核计划进行某种限制。
sentences.structure: 主句：they certainly point to some agreement coming together on four major pillars of a -- of some restraint on the nuclear program
sentences.analysis: 'point to' diikuti oleh frasa nomina. 'coming together' adalah frasa partisip sekarang yang berfungsi sebagai adjektiva yang menerangkan 'agreement'. Tanda pisah menunjukkan penjelasan.

sentences.en: But it's not a peace agreement.
sentences.cn: Tapi ini bukan perjanjian damai.
sentences.structure: Kalimat sederhana: But it's not a peace agreement
sentences.analysis: Konjungsi kontrastif But menghubungkan konteks.

sentences.en: It may be a ceasefire that opens up another negotiation.
sentences.cn: Ini mungkin adalah gencatan senjata yang membuka putaran negosiasi lain.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama: It may be a ceasefire; klausa relatif: that opens up another negotiation
sentences.analysis: 'that' memulai klausa relatif yang menerangkan 'ceasefire'. 'open up' berarti 'membuka'.

sentences.en: I think CNN -- by CNN's count, he has over the past three months declared on about 39 occasions -- not sure if that's exactly right -- that the war was over.
sentences.cn: Saya pikir CNN -- menurut hitungan CNN, dia dalam tiga bulan terakhir menyatakan sekitar 39 kali -- tidak yakin apakah angka itu tepat -- bahwa perang sudah berakhir.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama: I think; klausa objek: CNN -- by CNN's count, he has over the past three months declared on about 39 occasions -- not sure if that's exactly right -- that the war was over
sentences.analysis: Sisipan 'by CNN's count' dan 'not sure if that's exactly right' menginterupsi struktur kalimat. 'declare that' berarti 'menyatakan bahwa...'.

sentences.en: The president did say the most important thing was to get them to agree never to build a nuclear weapon.
sentences.cn: Presiden memang mengatakan bahwa hal terpenting adalah membuat mereka setuju untuk tidak pernah membuat senjata nuklir.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama: The president did say; klausa objek: the most important thing was to get them to agree never to build a nuclear weapon
sentences.analysis: 'did' menunjukkan penekanan. 'get sb. to do sth.' berarti 'membuat seseorang melakukan sesuatu'. 'agree never to do' berarti 'setuju untuk tidak pernah...'.

sentences.en: Well, of course, they've agreed to that many times before: when they signed the nuclear nonproliferation treaty; when they signed the agreement with President Obama 11 years ago.
sentences.cn: Yah, tentu saja, mereka sudah setuju dengan itu berkali-kali sebelumnya: ketika mereka menandatangani Traktat Nonproliferasi Nuklir; ketika mereka menandatangani perjanjian dengan Presiden Obama 11 tahun lalu.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama: they've agreed to that many times before; aposisi: when they signed the nuclear nonproliferation treaty; when they signed the agreement with President Obama 11 years ago
sentences.analysis: Dua klausa waktu yang diawali 'when' setelah titik dua berfungsi sebagai aposisi, memberikan contoh spesifik.

sentences.en: Another one that's in there is a 60-day ceasefire, quote, 'on all fronts.'
sentences.cn: Satu lagi yang ada di dalamnya adalah gencatan senjata 60 hari, kutipan, 'di semua lini.'
sentences.structure: Klausa utama: Another one that's in there is a 60-day ceasefire; sisipan: quote, 'on all fronts.'
sentences.analysis: 'that's in there' adalah klausa relatif yang menerangkan 'one'. 'quote' menunjukkan kutipan.

sentences.en: The fact that Israel's prime minister reportedly found out about this on social media, that really stands out to me, especially when we're talking about the potential ceasefire with Lebanon, with activity in Lebanon -- David.
sentences.cn: Fakta bahwa Perdana Menteri Israel dilaporkan mengetahui hal ini dari media sosial benar-benar menonjol bagi saya, terutama ketika kita berbicara tentang potensi gencatan senjata dengan Lebanon, dengan aktivitas di Lebanon -- David.
sentences.structure: Subjek: The fact that Israel's prime minister reportedly found out about this on social media; klausa utama: that really stands out to me; klausa waktu: especially when we're talking about the potential ceasefire with Lebanon, with activity in Lebanon
sentences.analysis: 'The fact that' memulai klausa aposisi. 'stand out' berarti 'menonjol, mencolok'. 'reportedly' adalah adverbia, berarti 'menurut laporan'.

sentences.en: The prime minister does not want to be part of this agreement and has made clear that he won't feel bound by a commitment that he didn't sign up to.
sentences.cn: Perdana Menteri tidak ingin menjadi bagian dari perjanjian ini dan telah menjelaskan bahwa dia tidak akan merasa terikat oleh komitmen yang tidak dia setujui.
sentences.structure: Predikat paralel: does not want to be part of this agreement and has made clear; klausa objek: that he won't feel bound by a commitment; klausa relatif: that he didn't sign up to
sentences.analysis: 'make clear that'意为'明确表示'。'feel bound by'表示'感到受……约束'。'sign up to'意为'签署、同意'。

topics.title: 特朗普宣布与伊朗达成'伟大和解'
topics.content: 特朗普声称与伊朗达成'伟大和解'，伊朗同意永不发展核武器，并可能于周末签署协议，重新开放霍尔木兹海峡。但伊朗外交部发言人表示尚未达成最终结论，以色列总理内塔尼亚胡通过社交媒体才得知消息。分析人士指出，这可能只是停火而非全面和平协议，且以色列不愿受此约束。
topics.keywords: great settlement · Strait of Hormuz · ceasefire · nuclear weapon · memorandum of understanding

topics.title: 鲁比奥将UFC比作登月引发争议
topics.content: 美国国务卿马可·鲁比奥在讲话中将UFC（终极格斗冠军赛）与肯尼迪总统的登月计划相提并论，称两者都看似不可能但最终实现。这一比喻引发争议，主持人质疑笼斗与外交的关系。UFC是综合格斗赛事，登月则是人类航天里程碑，两者性质迥异。
topics.keywords: Marco Rubio · UFC · Moon landing · cage fight · diplomacy

topics.title: SpaceX IPO与马斯克成为万亿富翁
topics.content: SpaceX今早在纳斯达克上市，进行首次公开募股（IPO）。埃隆·马斯克有望成为世界首位万亿富翁。SpaceX是私营航天公司，其IPO备受市场关注，被视为'IPO界的超级碗'。
topics.keywords: SpaceX · IPO · NASDAQ · Elon Musk · trillionaire

topics.title: 共和党质疑加州选举策略
topics.content: 共和党议员迈克·约翰逊批评加州选举策略，称其'在耍我们'，并呼吁在选举日当天进行投票。这反映了共和党对加州投票方式的持续不信任，可能引发特朗普试图控制州选举的担忧。
topics.keywords: Mike Johnson · California · voting tactics · election day · GOP

quiz.question:
原文中 'tout' 的含义是？
'Trump Touts 'Great Settlement' with Iran'
quiz.explanation: tout 意为'吹捧、兜售'，常用于新闻标题中表示某人积极宣传自己的观点或成就。这里指特朗普大力宣传与伊朗的'伟大和解'。

quiz.question:
原文中 'sticking point' 指的是？
'This has been a key sticking point, as we know, for Iran.'
quiz.explanation: sticking point 是固定搭配，意为'症结、关键分歧点'，指谈判中难以达成一致的问题。这里指伊朗在停火范围上的坚持。

quiz.question:
原文中 'pounding' 在军事语境中的含义是？
'38 days of pounding the Iranians'
quiz.explanation: pounding 在此处为名词，意为'猛烈打击、连续轰炸'，指军事上的持续攻击。

quiz.question: 根据原文，伊朗和以色列对特朗普宣布的'和解'反应如何？
options: B. 伊朗否认达成最终协议，以色列表示惊讶
C. 伊朗同意但以色列反对
quiz.explanation: 伊朗外交部发言人表示尚未达成最终结论，以色列总理内塔尼亚胡通过社交媒体才得知消息，显示双方均感意外。

quiz.question: 分析人士大卫·桑格认为特朗普的'和解'声明可能是什么？
options: B. 只是停火而非和平协议
quiz.explanation: 桑格指出这可能只是停火，开启另一轮谈判，而非结束战争的和平协议。

quiz.question: 关于SpaceX IPO，原文提到什么？
options: B. 马斯克将成为万亿富翁
C. 上市地点在纽约证券交易所
quiz.explanation: 原文明确说'Elon Musk now set to become the world's first trillionaire'，且SpaceX在纳斯达克上市。
